title: A Young Whale Gets Lost and Dies in a River
summary: A young gray whale named Willapa Willy swam far up a river in Washington state and sadly died. Scientists think it might have been hungry because it was thin. Gray whales travel long distances to find food in the Arctic, but sometimes they get lost or can't find enough to eat. This whale was first seen in the Willapa River and seemed okay, but it didn't swim back to the ocean. Other gray whales have also died recently, showing they are facing problems like not having enough food and getting hurt by ships. People love watching these big whales, but they need help to stay safe.

questions.question: How did the whale behave when first seen?

background_read: Gray whales are large ocean animals that can grow up to 15 meters long. They travel from warm waters to cold Arctic areas each spring to eat tiny creatures called prey. Sometimes, they get lost or can't find enough food, which makes them weak. Scientists study them to help protect them from dangers like ships and fishing gear. This story shows how one young whale got into trouble in a river, reminding us that whales need our care to survive.
Article_Structure: WHO: A young gray whale named Willapa Willy and scientists from the Cascadia Research Collective.
WHAT: The whale swam up a river, died, and scientists think hunger was a cause.
WHERE: In the Willapa River in Washington state, USA.
WHY: Gray whales are struggling to find food during migrations and face threats like ship strikes.

perspectives.description: Scientists study the whale's death to understand why gray whales are in trouble and how to help them.
